description: Apply the Central Limit Theorem — the sampling distribution of the sample mean is approximately normal with mean mu and variance sigma^2 / n for large n, regardless of population shape

core_theorem.statement: Given population with mean mu and finite variance sigma^2, the sampling distribution of X_bar from random samples of size n is approximately Normal(mu, sigma^2 / n) for large n
core_theorem.distributional_assumption: No distributional assumption on the population — only finite variance
core_theorem.large_n_threshold: Typically n >= 30 is considered sufficient for CLT to apply (convention, not theorem)

sampling_distribution_properties.standard_error: SE(X_bar) = sigma / sqrt(n) — known sigma; s / sqrt(n) when estimated
sampling_distribution_properties.convergence_rate: Standard error shrinks as 1/sqrt(n); halving error requires 4x sample size

implications.consistent_estimator: X_bar converges to mu as n -> infinity
implications.normal_approximation: Allows z-statistic inference for large n even if population is non-normal
implications.finite_variance_caveat: CLT fails if population variance is infinite (e.g., Cauchy distribution)

z_statistic.unknown_sigma_large_n: Z_approx = (X_bar - mu) / (s / sqrt(n)) ~ N(0, 1) when n is large

investment_applications.return_forecasting: Average realized return has SE = sigma / sqrt(T); tighter for longer history
investment_applications.performance_attribution: Test whether manager alpha differs from zero

validation.sample_size_large_enough: n >= 30 for reliable CLT approximation; smaller n requires known normality
validation.independence: Observations must be independent (or at least uncorrelated)

small_sample_warning.description: n < 30 — CLT may not hold; flag for t-distribution or distributional check

errors.message: Sample size must be at least 2 for the CLT to be applicable
Sample size is required; std dev (population or sample) needed for standard error

anti_patterns.rule: Do not apply the CLT to samples from fat-tailed distributions with infinite variance
anti_patterns.why: Stable distributions (e.g., Cauchy) violate the finite-variance requirement; sample mean does not converge to a normal distribution regardless of n

anti_patterns.rule: Do not conflate the population standard deviation with the standard error
anti_patterns.why: Sigma describes dispersion of individual values; SE describes dispersion of the sample mean — SE = sigma/sqrt(n) is smaller by factor sqrt(n)

anti_patterns.rule: Do not rely on n >= 30 as a universal threshold for CLT
anti_patterns.why: Heavily skewed or kurtotic populations may need n >> 30; the 30 rule is a convention, not a theorem. Check the sampling distribution empirically when in doubt

anti_patterns.rule: Do not apply the CLT to dependent (e.g., time-series) data without adjustment
anti_patterns.why: Autocorrelation inflates the effective sampling variance; use Newey-West or HAC standard errors for dependent observations

goals.description: Apply the Central Limit Theorem — the sampling distribution of the sample mean is approximately normal with mean mu and variance sigma^2 / n for large n, regardless of population shape

success_metrics.measurement: All decisions have complete audit trails

constraints.description: All operations must be auditable and traceable

tradeoffs.reason: trading operations require precise execution and full audit trails

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details

worked_example.interpretation: Sample mean is approximately Normal(0.08, 0.02^2); 95% CI half-width ~ 1.96 * 0.02 = 3.92%
